channel.title: Notes on Sheikyon
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on Sheikyon

item.title: On the Futility of Debate
item.description: Idealism aside, in general we debate not to change our ideas but to reinforce our convictions. The most sensible thing, in this sense, is to assume that the opponent is not someone who is interested in adopting one’s ideas. Whoever enters the battlefield wanting to fall before the other is because he is either suicidal or an imbecile. There is something noble, our democratic culture tells us, in the exchange of ideas and dialogue between those who think differently.
